Title: Thailand presses on with Cambodia border fence after 2025 clashes
Article:
PONG NAM RON: Thailand has completed the first section of a permanent border fence along parts of its frontier with Cambodia, the Thai military said on Wednesday, as tensions persist between the two Southeast Asian neighbours following two rounds of fighting last year.
Chief of Defence Forces General Ukrit Boontanon described the 1.3-km stretch of reinforced concrete panels topped with steel mesh and concertina wire in the eastern Pong Nam Ron district as "stable, strong and durable", saying it had been designed for long-term security.
"The concrete wall panels here have been test-fired. We have confirmed they can protect against small-arms fire," Ukrit told reporters during a media tour of the site.
Ukrit said the military planned to build more than 7 km of additional fencing connected to the existing section, while assessing other areas of the 817-km frontier where terrain and environmental conditions would allow similar construction.
"Each area has different conditions," he said. "We will build where it is beneficial and where it can be done without creating future problems."
Troops remain deployed on both sides of the Thailand-Cambodia border, where longstanding territorial disputes erupted into violent clashes with the use of air strikes and artillery in July and December last year, killing nearly 150 people and displacing 300,000.
The July outbreak was settled after five days ​following an intervention by US President Donald Trump, but clashes flared up again for 20 days in December before both sides agreed to a bilateral ceasefire.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Anwar says Malaysia will maintain ban on Israeli citizens entering country
Article:
SEREMBAN: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said Malaysia would maintain its policy of barring Israeli citizens from entering the country despite criticism from eight members of the United States Congress.
He said Malaysia, as an independent and sovereign nation, had long upheld the policy, stressing that it was not a new decision but one that had been in place for decades.
"Several members of the US Congress have issued statements criticising me over Malaysia's decision to bar Israeli citizens from entering the country, including from participating in companies and other activities.
"That will not deter us. They may issue threats, but I will firmly defend the rights of the Malaysian people and the decision made by Malaysians to continue prohibiting the entry of any Israeli citizen into Malaysia," he told reporters after the Rakan Muda Roadshow programme at the Paroi Youth and Sports Complex here today.
MORE TO  COME ...
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Mynews acquires RM24.7mil Rawang industrial land for new distribution centre
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: MyNews Holdings Bhd's wholly owned subsidiary Mynews Retail Sdn Bhd has entered into a sale and purchase agreement (SPA) with Thung Hing Metal Industry Sdn Bhd to acquire 13.5 acres leasehold industrial land in Rawang, Selangor for RM24.70 million.
The convenience store operator said in a Bursa Malaysia filing today that the land purchase with the lease set to expire on Jan 4, 2095 is a strategic investment to support its long-term operational and business expansion plans, particularly the construction of a new distribution centre.
It said the new distribution centre is needed as its existing facility is operating at constrained capacity with insufficient room for future growth.
"Securing the land now lets the company move ahead with construction, address capacity constraints and avoid rising land costs or the shrinking availability of suitable sites, with the new distribution centre expected to enhance supply chain efficiency, strengthen the company's asset base, and support continued business expansion over the medium to long term," it said.
MyNews said the purchase consideration was determined on a willing-buyer willing-seller basis after taking into account publicly available transaction data and indicative asking prices of comparable industrial land in the surrounding area.
The acquisition will be financed through a combination of internal funds and bank borrowings, with 15 per cent funded internally and the remainder via borrowings.
It added that the acquisition is not expected to affect MyNews' share capital and substantial shareholding structure or materially impact the company's earnings for the financial year ending Oct 31, 2026.
"Barring any unforeseen circumstances, the proposed acquisition is expected to be completed within 12 months from the date of the SPA," it said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: IGB Reit Q2 net profit rises to RM139.27mil on additional income, profit contribution
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: IGB Real Estate Investment Trust's (IGB REIT) net profit for the second quarter ended June 30, 2026 (Q2 2026) rose 50.6 per cent to RM139.27 million against RM92.50 million in the same quarter a year ago.
Revenue jumped to RM240.95 million, a 50.5 per cent increase from RM160.09 million in Q2 2025, while net property income was RM181.2 million, an increase of 51.1 per cent compared with the preceding year quarter's RM119.9 million, the company said in a filing with Bursa Malaysia today.
It said the higher total revenue, net property income, and profit after taxation were mainly due to additional income and profit contributed by Mid Valley Southkey, as well as higher rental income from Mid Valley Megamall and The Gardens Mall.
For the first half of 2026 (1H 2026), IGB REIT's net profit surged to RM302.08 million against RM199.08 million in 1H 2025, while revenue widened to RM502.29 million from RM331.53 million previously.
IGB REIT said it will remain focused on maintaining high occupancy, driving sustainable rental growth through proactive leasing strategies, preserving cost discipline and delivering an attractive retail experience for shoppers and tenants. "These initiatives are expected to support the REIT's long term resilience and sustainable value creation for unitholders," it added.
The REIT has declared an income distribution of 3.44 sen per unit, comprising 3.38 sen taxable and 0.06 sen non-taxable, for the financial period from April 1 to June 30, 2026, payable on Aug 21, 2026.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Investigators search Deutsche Bank's Frankfurt headquarters
Article:
FRANKFURT: German tax investigators were searching offices at Deutsche Bank's headquarters in Frankfurt on Wednesday as they investigate alleged tax fraud transactions, Die Welt and other media reported.
The bank confirmed that Duesseldorf prosecutors were at its office in the city, and that the probe related to transactions at its Postbank division between 2008 and 2010.
"Deutsche Bank is being searched as a third party in this matter and we are cooperating fully," Deutsche Bank said, declining to comment further.
Duesseldorf prosecutors did not immediately respond to a request for comment.
The probe relates to alleged tax fraud transactions known as cum-cum, according to media reports.
The alleged roles of German financial firms in these trades, as well as in related cum-ex deals, are likely to cost the industry around €7 billion (US$8 billion), German financial watchdog BaFin said last week, citing a survey.
Such schemes involved trading in stocks of German companies around dividend payout days, which authorities say amounted to tax fraud.
The transactions flourished during the financial crisis, and authorities estimated they stripped state coffers of billions.
A years-long crackdown has since sought to claw back the money.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Bursa Malaysia ends lower amid profit-taking, higher oil prices
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Bursa Malaysia closed lower on Wednesday as investors continued to take profit following the recent rally, while elevated crude oil prices and lingering geopolitical tensions in the Middle East kept market sentiment cautious, said an analyst.
At 5pm, the FTSE Bursa Malaysia KLCI (FBM KLCI) fell nine points, or 0.52  per cent, to 1,711.37 from Tuesday's close of 1,720.37.
The index opened 0.53 of a point easier at 1,719.84, and moved between 1,709.15 and 1,721.31 throughout the day.
On the broader market, decliners outnumbered advancers 590 to 450, while 596 counters were unchanged, 1,111 untraded, and 23 suspended.
Turnover narrowed to 3.43 billion units valued at RM2.50 billion from 3.48 billion units valued at RM2.79 billion on Tuesday.
Meanwhile, regional markets ended mixed as sentiment remained fragile, with elevated crude oil prices continuing to weigh on risk appetite after Yemen's Iran-aligned Houthi rebels threatened to widen the conflict in the Middle East.
Brent crude oil rose above US$94 per barrel after two tankers carrying Saudi crude reportedly reversed course in the Red Sea, underscoring the growing risk of disruptions to regional shipping routes, said Rakuten Trade Sdn Bhd vice-president of equity research Thong Pak Leng.
On the domestic front, he told Bernama that the recent pullback is a healthy consolidation following the FBM KLCI's strong advance from around 1,655 to 1,735.
"The sell-off may present bargain-hunting opportunities in fundamentally sound large-cap stocks, particularly as resilient domestic fundamentals and renewed foreign fund inflows continue to underpin the broader recovery.
"However, geopolitical uncertainties and volatile oil prices are likely to keep trading sentiment cautious," he said.
Hence, Thong expects the benchmark index to remain range-bound between 1,705 and 1,730 for the remainder of the week, with selective interest likely to centre on banking, energy, plantation, and other defensive counters.
Among the heavyweight counters, Maybank slipped eight sen to RM10.94, Public Bank shed five sen to RM5.15, Tenaga Nasional slid 28 sen to RM14.28, CIMB trimmed two sen to RM7.66, and IHH Healthcare shaved off 17 sen to RM8.33.
Among active stocks, Aimax and Nextgram Holdings inched up half a sen each to 1.5 sen and 4.5 sen, respectively, and Aimflex added three sen to 12 sen. Zetrix AI eased 1.5 sen to 71.5 sen, and Tanco Holdings decreased one sen to 30.5 sen.
Among top gainers, Malaysian Pacific Industries climbed 92 sen to RM47.76, United Plantations added 30 sen to RM34.04, Petronas Gas jumped 28 sen to RM17.68, and Kesm Industries rose 17 sen to RM4.25.
Nestle led the decliners, slipping RM1.60 to RM91.52, Hong Leong Industries dipped 44 sen to RM18.00, Batu Kawan sank 40 sen to RM20.90, and UMS Integration declined 29 sen to RM7.90.
Among the broader indices, the FBM Emas Shariah Index dropped 46.76 points to 12,497.69, the FBM Emas Index was 53.80 points lower at 12,655.96, the FBM Top 100 Index shrank 58.87 points to 12,696.03, the FBM Mid 70 Index decreased by 51.96 points to 17,956.81 and the FBM ACE Index inched down 5.84 points to 4,961.02.
By sector, the Financial Services Index slumped 93.02 points to 20,207.11, and the Plantation Index tumbled 35.77 points to 9,321.24, while the Industrial Products and Services Index perked up 1.12 points to 188.36, and the Energy Index gained 4.01 points to 774.41.
The Main Market volume improved to 1.94 billion units valued at RM2.22 billion compared to 1.86 billion units valued at RM2.48 billion on Tuesday.
Warrants turnover dwindled to 970.20 million units worth RM130.98 million versus 1.11 billion units worth RM138.80 million previously.
The ACE Market volume expanded to 520.43 million units valued at RM151.15 million from 496.93 million units valued at RM171.87 million yesterday.
Consumer products and services counters accounted for 151.97 million shares traded on the Main Market, industrial products and services (427.01 million), construction (97.06 million), technology (693.81 million), financial services (70.93 million), property (197.46 million), plantation (30.53 million), real estate investment trusts (13.16 million), closed-end fund (8,400), energy (118.43 million), healthcare (55.90 million), telecommunications and media (19.05 million), transportation and logistics (28.92 million), utilities (36.35 million), and business trusts (9,600).

Title: Sabah Native Courts Bill strengthens legal framework for customary justice
Article:
KOTA KINABALU: The newly passed Native Courts Bill provides a more comprehensive, modern and structured legal framework to strengthen the Native Court institution.
State Executive Council Member for Religious Affairs Datuk Mohd Arifin Mohd Arif said the existing enactment had been in force since 1992.
"After more than three decades, comprehensive reform is necessary to ensure that the customary justice system meets present-day needs without compromising the roots, identity and unique customs of Sabah's Native communities.
"What is being reformed is the institution and administrative procedures of customary justice, so that customs can continue to be preserved and implemented through a court system that is more organised, fair and credible," he said in his winding-up speech.
Mohd Arifin, who is also the Local Government and Housing Minister, said the Bill streamlines the hierarchy of the Native Courts through the establishment of the Native District Court, Native High Court and Native Court of Appeal, providing clearer definitions of each court level.
In terms of judicial appointments, the Bill introduces the Native Judicial Appointments Advisory Committee to promote transparency based on merit and qualifications.
"The role of Tetua (customary elders) continues to be recognised, as their knowledge of customary law is an essential element in the administration of customary justice.
"Through the establishment of a Register of Tetua, their appointment and participation can be carried out in a more uniform, orderly and merit-based manner."
Earlier, appointed assemblyman Datuk Roger Chin said the Bill was far more than a replacement for the Native Courts Enactment 1992, as the reforms demonstrated that the government had approached the exercise not merely as an administrative review.
"It is a serious effort to strengthen the institutional foundations upon which Native Customary Law will be administered for generations to come."
Datuk Donald Mojuntin (Upko-Moyog) said the decision and documents issued by Native Courts should be recognised by all federal government agencies, particularly the Immigration Department and National Registration Department.
He said there were couples who had tied the knot through the Native Courts but were later advised to remarry under civil law solely to fulfil administrative requirements.
Datuk Seri Wilfred Madius Tangau (Upko-Tamparuli) also proposed the inclusion of a new subsection to expressly provide that the conversion of one spouse to another religion does not, by itself, terminate the jurisdiction of the Native Court over a marriage solemnised under customary law.
"This amendment would provide legal certainty that the Native Court continues to have jurisdiction over marriages solemnised under customary law.
"The conversion of one spouse should not automatically extinguish the Native Court's jurisdiction. The purpose of this proposal is not to create overlapping jurisdictions, but to ensure that justice remains accessible to all parties."
Isnaraissah Munirah Majilis (Warisan-Usukan) proposed that comprehensive guidelines be developed to serve as a central reference for all parties due to Sabah's diverse backgrounds.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: N9 polls: Candidate appeal key to razor-thin Juasseh contest
Article:
Slug: izbattle
HL: N9 polls: Juasseh emerges as Kuala Pilah's closest battleground
By Luqman Hakim
KUALA PILAH: Juasseh is shaping up to be one of the closest contests in Kuala Pilah, with BN and PH offering contrasting assessments of the race for a seat that BN retained by just 78 votes in the 2023 Negri Sembilan election.
The three-cornered contest involving BN, PH and Bersatu has prompted both major contenders to argue that their respective strengths will resonate with voters.
BN candidate Datuk Ismail Lasim believes Bersatu's decision to contest under its own logo, instead of the PN symbol, could work in BN's favour.
He said voters were more familiar with the PN logo than Bersatu's party symbol, potentially giving BN a slight advantage.
Ismail also said that the political landscape had shifted since the last state election, when BN contested as part of the unity government.
He said some Malay voters were uncomfortable with BN's cooperation with PH in 2023 and believed contesting independently this time could help the coalition regain support.
The former Juasseh assemblyman said his previous service in the constituency from 2018 to 2023 and familiarity with local communities would also work in his favour.
He described Juasseh as a constituency with no major development issues, citing rapid residential growth, and said his focus would instead be on improving residents' well-being and expanding educational assistance.
In the 2023 Negri Sembilan election, BN candidate Bibi Sharliza Mohd Khalid, the wife of former Menteri Besar Tan Sri Mohd Isa Samad, retained the seat with a narrow 78-vote majority over the late Datuk Eddin Syazlee Shith of PN.
This time, however, the contest has taken on a different complexion with a three-cornered fight involving BN's Ismail, PH's Mohd Aidil Abdullah and Bersatu's Mohd Zuhaimi Md Yusof.
The three candidates are vying for the support of 13,671 registered voters in the constituency.
Meanwhile, PH candidate Mohd Aidil Abdullah admitted his campaign's internal voter canvassing showed him trailing BN by about 200 votes.
However, he insisted the contest remained wide open, saying his campaign was intensifying house-to-house visits to win over undecided voters.
"We are still behind in our internal estimates, but there is still time. Every day we continue meeting voters and identifying those who are still undecided," he said.
Aidil, who described himself as the underdog against two experienced rivals, said voters were increasingly judging candidates on accessibility and grassroots service rather than party affiliation.
The former Juasseh coordinator said his work with residents during the Covid-19 pandemic had helped him build close ties with the community.
He also argued that the end of the previous BN-PH cooperation had left some voters confused, making candidates' appeal more important than party loyalty.
Universiti Malaya political analyst, Associate Professor Dr Mohammad Tawfik Yaakub, said voters in Negri Sembilan were unlikely to decide based solely on party or candidate, but on a combination of both.
He said party identity remained important, particularly among loyal supporters, but candidate quality would be the deciding factor in rural, semi-urban and marginal seats.
Based on his preliminary observations, Tawfik said voters were placing greater emphasis on state and local issues, with approachable candidates who understood local concerns, had proven service records and enjoyed community trust more likely to attract fence-sitters.
He said 11 seats in the 2023 state election were won with majorities of fewer than 1,000 votes, making candidate selection increasingly critical.
"In constituencies like these, a candidate perceived as an outsider can become a liability for the party, while a local candidate with close community ties may secure the additional votes needed for victory.
"Voters today are more discerning. They are not only looking at the party logo but also whether the candidate is accessible, has delivered on public service and is committed to solving everyday local issues."
Ends
Keywords: news, NST, Malaysia, Kuala Pilah, Juasseh, Negri Sembilan, BN, PH, Bersatu, by-election, voters, politics
From: Luqman
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: 108 undocumented migrants detained in Johor immigration raid
Article:
JOHOR BARU: A total of 108 undocumented migrants believed to have been working at business premises in Taman Sri Bahagia here were detained last night.
They were arrested in a joint operation by the Johor Immigration Department and the Malaysia Border Control and Protection Agency.
The operation, which began at 8pm, was carried out following intelligence gathering and surveillance, targeting business premises believed to be operated by foreigners.
Johor Immigration director Datuk Mohd Rusdi Mohd Darus said the operation was aimed at strengthening enforcement of immigration laws and curbing the abuse of visit passes, work passes and immigration facilities.
"Officers checked the travel documents, passes and permits of individuals at the identified premises to ensure they were legally present in Malaysia and carrying out activities in accordance with the law.
"Following the inspections, 108 foreigners aged between 18 and 49 were detained for various immigration offences," he said in a statement today.
Those detained comprised 101 men — 32 Bangladeshis, 22 Myanmar nationals, 16 Pakistanis, 15 Nepalese, 12 Indonesians and four Indians — as well as seven women comprising three Myanmar nationals, two Indonesians and two Nepalese.
Rusdi said all those detained were suspected of committing offences under the Immigration Act 1959/63 and the Immigration Regulations 1963.
They have been placed at the Setia Tropika Immigration Depot for further action.
He said two notices, which serve as summonses to witnesses, were also issued during the operation to assist in the investigation.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Bank Negara's international reserves at US$131.8bil as at July 15
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Bank Negara Malaysia's international reserves stood at US$131.8 billion as at July 15, 2026, compared with US$132.6 billion as at the end of last month.
In a statement today, it said the reserves position is sufficient to finance 4.7 months of imports of goods and services, and is 0.9 times of total short-term external debt.
Bank Negara reported that the gross international reserves included foreign currency reserves (US$116.4 billion), the International Monetary Fund (IMF) reserve position (US$1.3 billion), special drawing rights (SDRs) (US$6.0 billion), gold (US$5.8 billion), and other reserve assets (US$2.3 billion).
Total assets amounted to RM633.97 billion, comprising mainly gold, foreign financial assets and other reserves including SDRs (RM533.93 billion), Malaysian government papers (RM12.68 billion), deposits with financial institutions (RM2.99 billion), loans and advances (RM20.34 billion), property and equipment (RM4.55 billion) and other assets (RM59.49 billion).
Capital and liabilities totalled RM633.97 billion, comprising capital (RM100 million), reserves (RM197.62 billion), currency in circulation (RM183.24 billion), deposits by financial institutions (RM113.06 billion), federal government deposits (RM16.25 billion), other deposits (RM85.63 billion), Bank Negara papers (RM7.75 billion), allocation of SDRs (RM26.71 billion), and other liabilities (RM3.61 billion).
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Anwar urges Negri voters to keep state aligned with Putrajaya
Article:
SEREMBAN: Pakatan Harapan chairman Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim today urged Negri Sembilan voters to continue giving the coalition's mandate to govern the state, saying political stability was essential to ensure continued development and safeguard the people's welfare.
He said close cooperation between the state and federal governments would enable development initiatives to continue benefiting the people.
"We are developing the state while looking after the people. What Tok Min (caretaker Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Aminuddin Harun) has achieved in Negri Sembilan over the past three years is evident to all, including in the management of the state's resources.
"So, I ask for your cooperation in ensuring political stability at both the federal and state levels, with both governments working in the same direction.
"Make your choice so that the state government remains aligned with the federal government, enabling us to do our best for the people," he said in his speech at the Program Santai Petang Warga Ampangan today.
Anwar acknowledged that the government was not without shortcomings and pledged to continue improving its performance.
"We are not saying everything we have done is perfect. As candidates and leaders, we will continue to identify weaknesses and make improvements where necessary," he said.
He said the implementation of the Sungai Batang Penar river maintenance project demonstrated the PH-led state government's commitment to ensuring development gains and state resources were returned to the people.
Anwar said the reciprocal river maintenance approach implemented in Ampangan not only helped address flooding but also ensured related resources were managed transparently for the benefit of the community.
Under the initiative, proceeds from sand dredging are channelled back to finance river maintenance works and infrastructure development.
"In the past, those who dredged the sand made the money by taking and selling it. Now, the government requires them to dredge the sand and pay back. That is the difference," he said.
Anwar also urged voters to make informed choices when electing their representatives.
"That is why, when we choose our representatives or a political party, we should not only think, 'They will look after the Malays, they will look after the Chinese, they will look after the Indians.' In the end, they only look after themselves.
"So, I ask everyone to make the right decision. Do not take this lightly. Based on my experience, choose people with dignity and integrity. God willing, our future will be more secure."
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: 'We sat down for lunch': Fahmi dismisses talk of Cabinet rift
Article:
KUALA PILAH: The Cabinet remains united despite recent public exchanges over ministerial discipline during the Negri Sembilan election campaign, Communications Minister Datuk Seri Fahmi Fadzil said today.
Responding to Barisan Nasional chairman Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi's remarks that Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim could dismiss BN ministers if dissatisfied with their performance, Fahmi said Anwar's recent warning was simply a reminder for members of the administration to uphold discipline and professionalism.
"What the Prime Minister said was a reminder to all Cabinet members to maintain discipline and continue acting professionally during the campaign, without breaching ethics and discipline.
"It applies not only to ministers and deputy ministers, but also to those heading or chairing government agencies," he told reporters during a campaign visit to Felda Kepis with Senaling candidate Mohd Hanis Mohd Alimin.
Fahmi said today's Cabinet meeting proceeded smoothly, with ministers continuing to demonstrate good working relations.
"There were no issues that could not be discussed. In fact, after the Cabinet meeting, we all sat down and had lunch together.
"We can see that relations at the leadership level are very good," he said.
He said the federal government's priority remained steering the country through global economic uncertainty arising from renewed conflict in the Middle East, stressing that political stability was essential to sustaining economic growth.
On Sunday, Anwar warned that ministers, deputy ministers, and heads of federal agencies must resign if they used their positions to attack fellow unity government partners during state election campaigns.
Anwar said while parties aligned at the federal level were free to contest against one another in state elections, those holding federal office must maintain discipline and avoid turning their positions into political platforms.
In response, Foreign Minister Datuk Seri Mohamad Hasan said he was ready to step down from his cabinet post if ordered to do so by the prime minister.
Mohamad, who is also BN deputy chairman, added that no BN member had criticised the federal government throughout the campaign period so far.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Police hunt two men over violent robbery of elderly woman in Lenggong
Article:
GERIK: Police are hunting two men suspected of robbing and assaulting an elderly woman at her home in Kampung Selat Pagar, Lenggong, early yesterday.
District police chief Superintendent Abdul Samad Othman said the victim in her 80s, who lives alone, was attacked after the suspects broke into her house at 5.10am.
He said the pair gained entry by prying open the front door before one of them pushed and kicked the victim while threatening her with an iron rod.
"The suspects fled with several of the victim's belongings after the robbery.
"Preliminary investigations found that they escaped in a dark-coloured car bearing the registration number VMG 8433, which is believed to be using a fake registration plate," he said in a statement today.
Abdul Samad said police have launched a manhunt for the suspects and are pursuing several leads to identify and locate them.
"We urge members of the public with information on the incident or the suspects to come forward and contact the nearest police station to assist in the investigation," he said.
The case is being investigated under Sections 395 and 397 of the Penal Code for gang robbery and gang robbery with the use of a weapon, which carry a jail term of up to 20 years and whipping upon conviction.
Earlier, a video of the incident, which has since gone viral, showed the two suspects barging into the house before one of them kicked the elderly woman in the chest, causing her to fall to the ground.
She could be heard repeatedly calling for help.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Malaysia, US discuss trade ties amid tariff uncertainty
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia and the United States have discussed ways to strengthen bilateral trade ties, including ongoing Sections 301 and 232 investigations that could affect Malaysian exports.
Investment, Trade and Industry Minister Datuk Seri Johari Abdul Ghani said Malaysia hopes any trade measures introduced by the US would take into account the shared interests of both countries.
Such measures should complement their respective economies and support stable and resilient global supply chains, he said in a Facebook post following his working visit to Washington.
Johari said he raised the matter during his maiden bilateral meeting with US Trade Representative Jamieson Greer.
"I highlighted Malaysia's strategic role as a trusted trading partner and an important contributor to global supply chains, particularly in high-technology manufacturing," he said.
Johari said both countries agreed to continue resolving trade-related issues through constructive dialogue, closer engagement and transparent information sharing.
"These efforts are important to safeguard Malaysia's trade and investment interests while reinforcing our position as a stable, trusted and competitive economic partner," he said.
The US is set to impose new tariffs on dozens of countries as President Donald Trump's temporary global tariff suspension expires this week.
According to AFP, the Trump administration has prepared additional tariffs targeting 60 trading partners over alleged failures to address forced labour, as officials seek to revive the president's trade agenda following legal challenges.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Anwar posts pantun stressing unity and consensus amid state election campaign
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim today shared a message on unity and consensus, posting a four-line Malay pantun underscoring the value of friendship and solidarity.
In a post on his official Facebook page, Anwar wrote:
"Burung merbah terbang sekawan, Guruh berdentum membelah awan, Berbeza langkah, jangan putus kawan, Selagi muafakat, kita sehaluan."
The post was accompanied by the evergreen 1980s hit song "Madu dan Racun", lending added resonance to the message.
Anwar also shared photographs from a Cabinet meeting attended by, among others, Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi and Transport Minister Anthony Loke, all appearing relaxed and in good spirits.
The post comes as public attention remains focused on the relationship between Pakatan Harapan and Barisan Nasional, which are contesting against each other in the Negri Sembilan state election despite being partners in the federal government.
While the two coalitions are competing on the campaign trail, the post has been widely viewed as a reminder to preserve the stability and cohesion of the federal government.
In the Negeri Sembilan election, PH, chaired by Anwar, is contesting all 36 state seats, while BN, led by Ahmad Zahid, is fielding candidates in 25 constituencies besides forging a pact with Perikatan Nasional.
The Negri Sembilan State Assembly was dissolved on June 5, and the Election Commission has set July 28 for early voting and Aug 1 for polling.
Anwar's post drew an enthusiastic online response, attracting more than 11,500 likes within two hours of being published.
The comments section was flooded with more than 1,700 responses, with some users replying in pantun, while many praised the prime minister's conciliatory tone and statesmanship.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Ousted Bangladesh PM Hasina vows to return despite death sentence
Article:
DHAKA: Bangladesh's ousted Prime Minister Sheikh Hasina has vowed to return home and appear in court despite the risk of imprisonment or execution, while rejecting her conviction over a 2024 deadly crackdown on student-led protests as "politically motivated."
"I will return to Bangladesh. I will appear before the court," said Hasina, 78, in a recent written interview with Kyodo News, without identifying a specific court.
"They may send me to prison. They may even try to hang me. I know those risks," she said, apparently referring to Bangladesh's authorities.
After resigning and fleeing Bangladesh in August 2024 amid the protests triggered by a court ruling on the civil servant job quota system, Hasina has lived in India.
She was tried in absentia and sentenced to death by Bangladesh's war crimes tribunal in November 2025 for crimes against humanity connected to the crackdown.
Bangladesh has requested India to extradite Hasina. A spokesperson for India's Ministry of External Affairs recently said New Delhi had received the request and was examining it in light of the legal and judicial processes involved.
Responding to a media report earlier this month about Hasina's intention to return, Zahed Ur Rahman, information adviser to Prime Minister Tarique Rahman, said at a July 14 press briefing that the government was seeking to bring her back to face trial and would ensure her an opportunity to defend herself.
After the high court in June 2024 reinstated a 30 percent quota for descendants of veterans of the 1971 independence war, student-led protests began and later escalated into a broader anti-government movement. Protesters clashed with security forces and supporters of Hasina's then-ruling Awami League.
The United Nations estimated as many as 1,400 people were killed during the unrest, most of them shot by security forces. Hasina denied ordering the killings and claimed the movement was manipulated by political opponents.
"If independent foreign forensic experts examine how each person died (during the unrest) and from which weapons or bullets, the truth can still be found," she said.
Regarding the 2025 ruling by the war crimes tribunal, Human Rights Watch has raised concerns about the trial held in the absence of Hasina, while stressing those responsible for abuses under her government should be held to account.
Hasina, daughter of Bangladesh's founding father Sheikh Mujibur Rahman, served as prime minister for a total of five terms from June 1996 to July 2001 and from January 2009 to August 2024.
During Hasina's third term, militants attacked the Holey Artisan Bakery in Dhaka in July 2016, killing 20 hostages – nine Italians, seven Japanese, two Bangladeshis, one Indian and one US citizen of Bangladeshi origin.
Two police officers were also killed while responding to the attack.
Seven Islamist militants were sentenced to death in 2019 for their roles in the attack, later reduced to life imprisonment.
The Japanese victims were working on a rapid railway system project in the Bangladeshi capital assisted by the Japan International Cooperation Agency.
"A stable Bangladesh is not only important for Bangladesh. It matters for South Asia, the Bay of Bengal, development partners and countries like Japan that have invested trust, resources and friendship in our development journey," Hasina said, according to Kyodo News.
Speaking of Bangladesh's future, she said, "The country needs healing. But reconciliation cannot mean impunity," adding that those responsible for crimes must face proper legal processes.
After Hasina fled Bangladesh, an interim government was formed under Nobel Peace Prize laureate Muhammad Yunus, who was appointed chief adviser after student protest leaders proposed him for the post.
Following a general election in February 2026 that was won by the opposition Bangladesh Nationalist Party, the party's leader Tarique Rahman took office as prime minister.
All activities of Hasina's Awami League party have been banned since May 2025.
"The next decade can be a decade of recovery and renewal, but only if democracy is restored and the people are allowed to decide their own future," she said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Court upholds RM5.62mil negligence award in ex-Prolintas CEO medical negligence case
Article:
PUTRAJAYA: The Court of Appeal recently affirmed the High Court's ruling that a private hospital and two doctors were liable for medical negligence that left former CEO of highway operator, Prolintas Datuk Zainudin A. Kadir with permanent brain damage following routine sinus surgery.
A three-member bench chaired by Datuk N. P. Ravinthran made the ruling after unanimously dismissed appeals by Pusat Pakar Tawakal Sdn Bhd, ear, nose and throat (ENT) surgeon, and anaesthetist finding that they were liable for medical negligence.
The court also increased the damages awarded to Zainudin's estate from RM5.1 million to RM5.62 million after allowing part of a cross-appeal by his widow, Datin Nor Rizam Abdul Wahab, who is the administrator of his estate.
The additional award comprised RM504,527.69 in hospital and medical expenses and RM16,960 in legal costs incurred to obtain a court order appointing a committee to manage Zainudin's affairs after he suffered permanent brain damage.
Other members of the bench were Datuk Azhahari Kamal Ramli and Datuk Ahmad Fairuz Zainol Abidin.
According to the case facts, Zainudin was admitted to the hospital on Feb 22, 2016, and underwent nasal surgery four days later. The surgery began at 5.45pm and ended at 8.05pm, before he collapsed at 9.30pm.
Zainudin died in 2021 during the Covid-19 pandemic.
Azhahari who wrote the grounds of judgment said Zainudin's brain injury resulted from "an unbroken chain of events" beginning with his premature discharge from the recovery room after surgery and culminating in a series of failures during the emergency response in the ward.
He said although the patient had been adequately informed of the risks of the surgery and had given informed consent, negligence occurred after the operation.
The judge said Zainudin was transferred from the recovery room to the ward after only about 25 minutes despite the hospital's own protocol requiring patients to remain under observation for at least one hour after receiving reversal medication.
He said Zainudin was still drowsy after receiving powerful anaesthetic drugs and had not been properly assessed for airway control.
The court also found that doctors and nurses failed to properly monitor his breathing after surgery and that it was his son, who is also a doctor, who first noticed he had stopped breathing and alerted hospital staff.
"The ENT surgeon had failed to conduct a correct intubation on the patient, causing further delay in supplying oxygen to the lung, a vital respiratory organ of the patient."
The court also noted that the hospital failed to ensure essential emergency equipment was available during the Code Blue response.
"The hospital is responsible for ensuring that it has sufficient equipment for use during an emergency.
"The hospital must also be held liable for the failure of the nurses to maintain a proper medical record for the patient.
"The hospital should not be absolved from any liability arising from the negligent act of the doctors," added the court.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Penang police seize RM470k worth of ecstasy, arrest man
Article:
GEORGE TOWN: Penang police seized 3.1kg of MDMA worth about RM470,000 in a raid at Teluk Air Tawar on Sunday.
They also arrested a 37-year-old man during the raid.
State police chief Datuk Dennis Lim said the raid was carried out by the state Narcotics Criminal Investigation Department at a bungalow in Teluk Air Tawar, Butterworth.
"The suspect has been remanded and is being investigated under Section 39B of the Dangerous Drugs Act 1952," he told a press conference at the northeast district police headquarters here today.
Lim said investigations were ongoing to determine the extent of the suspect's involvement and identify other individuals linked to the case.
He did not disclose further details on the source of the drugs or whether the suspect was part of a larger trafficking syndicate, saying investigations were still in progress.
The narcotics case was among eight major cases announced by Lim during the press conference, which also covered criminal investigation, commercial crime and enforcement operations.
Lim urged members of the public to continue providing information to police on drug trafficking and other criminal activities.
"Public cooperation is important. We urge anyone with information to come forward so that police can take the necessary action," he added.
MDMA, short for methylenedioxymethamphetamine, is a synthetic drug commonly known as ecstasy.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: RM27mil-a-day World Cup betting syndicate busted, 161 held
Article:
SUBANG JAYA: Police have crippled an online gambling syndicate operating under the guise of legitimate businesses, including a computer company, after raids on 19 premises across the Klang Valley led to 161 arrests.
The syndicate, at the height of the World Cup, was collecting RM27 million in bets daily.
Bukit Aman Criminal Investigation Department director Datuk M. Kumar said the suspects, detained during Op Soga XI last Thursday, comprised 106 men and 55 women, including six foreigners, aged between 19 and 62.
Among those arrested were five local men believed to be company directors and shareholders linked to the syndicate.
"We believe one of these people is the mastermind," he told a press conference at the Subang Jaya district police headquarters today.
Police also seized 182 desktop computers, 137 laptops, 152 mobile phones and other equipment believed to have been used to operate and promote online gambling activities.
"The operation was carried out by the Bukit Aman CID with the assistance of the Kuala Lumpur and Selangor CID contingents, as well as the Malaysian Communications and Multimedia Commission (MCMC).
"Initial investigations found that the syndicate developed its own application and promoted it to customers, allowing them to place bets on football matches and various other forms of online gambling," Kumar said.
He added that although the application was used by customers both locally and abroad, the entire betting operation was controlled by the syndicate from Malaysia.
"Police estimate that the syndicate handled betting credit transactions worth about RM27 million daily.
"Based on our investigation they collected the most bets when the World Cup entered its second week onward," he said.
Kumar said investigators believed that although the companies were registered under different names, they were all linked to the same syndicate.
He added that the group was not solely focused on football betting but also offered a wide range of other gambling activities.
Police have opened 14 investigation papers involving cases in Kuala Lumpur and Selangor under Section 4C and Section 4E of the Betting Act 1953, Section 4G of the Common Gaming Houses Act 1953, and Section 120B of the Penal Code.
Separately, Kumar said that since Op Soga began on June 11 until July 20, police had carried out 649 raids nationwide, arresting 945 people comprising 835 men and 110 women.
During the same period, police seized nearly RM200,000 in cash and uncovered betting transactions worth RM35.15 million.
Working with the MCMC, police also identified 637 websites promoting online gambling, with some already blocked while the remainder are undergoing further action.
ENDS
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Senegal's 'Pink Lake' recovers its rosy hue, reviving salt trade
Article:
STANDING waist-deep in the waters of Senegal's famed Pink Lake, Seydou Toure uses a shovel to scrape salt from the lakebed that he will later sell to tourists and traders.
It's a way of life that sustained him for more than a decade and a half before flooding in 2022 altered the ecosystem, robbing the lake of the salinity that both paid Toure's bills and gave the water its rosy hue.
Overnight, salt harvesters like Toure lost their jobs and tourism slowed to a trickle, devastating the local economy.
Now that painful chapter appears to be ending: After a yearslong effort to pump excess water from the lake, its old colour is back, and community leaders earlier this month held a traditional ceremony marking its official re-opening for salt extraction.
Pink Lake, officially known as Lake Retba, gets its colour from microorganisms such as the Dunaliella salina algae that thrive in highly saline conditions, according to geologist Cheikh Ibrahima Youm of Cheikh Anta Diop University in Dakar.
Roughly 3,000 salt harvesters normally work at the lake located 30 km northeast of Dakar, according to Ibrahima Mbaye, president of a local NGO dedicated to protecting the lake.
Nearly 2,000 additional people depend indirectly on the industry through activities like transport and trade, he said.
Most years, the lake can also attract between 7,000 and 8,000 visitors during the tourist season, which runs from October to May, he said.
But in August 2022, during a period of severe flooding in Dakar, authorities diverted floodwaters into Pink Lake, using it for emergency drainage because it is in a low-lying area, Mbaye said.
[[nid:1494847]]
The influx of freshwater diluted the lake's highly saline waters, disrupting both salt formation and the conditions needed for its famous pink colour, Youm said.
"We woke up one morning, and we had lost everything," Toure said.
Associations representing salt producers, tour operators, artisans and local businesses immediately got to work raising around 23 million CFA francs (around US$40,000) to fund pumping operations aimed at removing excess water from the lake, Mbaye said.
The money was used to rent pumps, buy fuel and install roughly 600 metres of piping to drain water towards the sea, he said.
Around 100 volunteers helped install, operate and monitor the pumping system, Mbaye said, adding that local authorities later helped by providing a larger pump and electricity.
As salinity gradually returned, so did the lake's colour.
Local hotels are now preparing for tourists to follow suit, and Toure is once again spending hours in the water extracting salt.
"The work is physically demanding," he said, rubbing shea butter onto his skin for protection.
But he has no qualms with his chosen career.
"The money I earned from this salt allowed me to buy land, horses and cattle. Everything comes from the lake," he said.
"If someone wanted to become a salt harvester, I would tell them not to hesitate... it's worth it."
* The writer is from Reuters
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Career diplomat Joshua Harris nominated as next US ambassador to Malaysia
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The United States has nominated career diplomat Joshua Harris to serve as the next Ambassador to Malaysia.
Harris is a career member of the Senior Foreign Service and currently holds the rank of Minister-Counselor.
The statement regarding Harris' nomination was posted on the
White House
official website.
Online checks reveal that Harris is a seasoned diplomat whose career spans several decades of high-level leadership and extensive international representation.
Harris previously served as Deputy Assistant Secretary of State for Near Eastern Affairs, overseeing US diplomacy toward Algeria, Egypt, Libya, Morocco and Tunisia, and was nominated as US Ambassador to Algeria in 2024, according to the U.S. Department of State. https://www.state.gov/biographies/joshua-harris
He has also served as Chargé d'Affaires at the US Embassy in Iraq, Deputy Chief of Mission in Tripoli, Libya, and in postings in Ljubljana, Baghdad and Zagreb.
He is a graduate of Georgetown University's Edmund A. Walsh School of Foreign Service and the London School of Economics.
The ambassadorial post in Kuala Lumpur has been vacant since
Edgard Kagan
concluded his tenure in February, with career diplomat David Gamble serving as Chargé d'Affaires in the interim.
Kagan had earlier bid farewell to Malaysia, saying the two countries had built a partnership "stronger, more resilient and effective than ever."
The nomination will require confirmation by the US Senate before Harris can formally take up the post.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Johor's first deputy excos hit the ground running
Article:
ISKANDAR PUTERI: Johor's inaugural deputy executive councillors have pledged to hit the ground running, outlining priorities ranging from attracting investments and tackling social issues to empowering youths following their appointments today.
Speaking after the announcement of the state's first deputy exco line-up at Kota Iskandar, the newly appointed deputy excos said they were ready to support the Johor government in accelerating policy implementation while ensuring development reached communities across the state.
Deputy Exco for Investment, Trade, Consumer Affairs and Human Resources Syed Hussein Syed Abdullah, 42, described his appointment as a major responsibility that would require close cooperation to realise the Maju Johor 2030 agenda.
"This is a huge responsibility, and it requires the commitment of everyone to ensure the goals of Maju Johor 2030 are achieved.
"We want every investment brought into Johor to generate wider economic spillover for Bangsa Johor, including communities in rural districts and areas that have received less development."
Deputy Exco for Women, Family and Community Development Chan San San, 46, said her experience dealing with social issues would enable her to contribute meaningfully in her new role.
She said her exposure to cases involving domestic violence, child abuse and community welfare had given her a deeper understanding of the challenges faced by vulnerable groups.
"I hope to use my experience to help find the best solutions to the social issues affecting our communities.
"We must assess every challenge comprehensively and ensure that the measures we take genuinely benefit those who need assistance."
Deputy Exco for Youth and Sports, Entrepreneur Development and Cooperatives Nadhirah Afiqah Abdul Rahim, 32, said the portfolio closely reflected her longstanding involvement in youth development programmes.
She said working directly with young people had given her valuable insight into their aspirations and needs.
"This portfolio is very close to my heart because I have long been involved in youth programmes at the grassroots level.
"I will give my full commitment and work closely with Exco Mohd Hairi Mad Shah to strengthen Johor's youth, entrepreneurship and cooperative development agenda."
The appointments form part of Johor's first-ever deputy exco structure, introduced by Menteri Besar Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi to strengthen the government's administrative capacity and improve the delivery of policies and programmes.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Japan cities 'cruelly hot' for first time
Article:
TOKYO: Japanese cities are experiencing "cruelly hot days", a new meteorological term devised this year to warn people of intense heat above 40C, with heatstroke alerts issued across almost the whole country.
Three central cities on Tuesday logged scorching heat above the threshold, called "kokushobi" which translates to "cruelly hot day", according to the Japan Meteorological Agency.
On Wednesday, swathes of other areas, including the central hub of Nagoya and communities near Tokyo in the east, were enduring temperatures approaching or exceeding 40C.
The agency has issued heatstroke alerts in 41 out of Japan's 47 prefectures, urging the public – especially the many elderly – to take steps to stay cool.
In the rural Tochigi region, north of Tokyo, an 85-year-old man was found collapsed inside the greenhouse of his farm on Tuesday. He was later confirmed dead, a local fire spokeswoman told AFP.
"The greenhouse was extremely hot inside," she said.
The Fire and Disaster Management Agency on Wednesday urged the public to regard the extreme heat as a "disaster."
"Temperatures have been rising since mid-July, and severe heat is expected to continue nationwide," the agency said.
"It is no exaggeration to call this extreme heat a 'disaster' with many people being rushed to hospitals or even dying as a result of heatstroke," it said.
Scientists say human-induced climate change is increasing the frequency and intensity of extreme heat events in Japan and elsewhere.
Last year, the country sweltered through its hottest summer on record.
Japan's highest recorded temperature was recorded on August 5, 2025 when it hit 41.8C in Isesaki north of Tokyo.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Police seek William to assist Penang phone scam probe
Article:
GEORGE TOWN: Police are seeking a local man known as William to assist investigations into a phone scam syndicate uncovered in Bukit Mertajam recently.
State police chief Datuk Dennis Lim said William, 49, whose last known address is in Kluang, Johor, is being sought only as a witness.
"We are looking for him to assist in our investigations. His name surfaced during the probe, so we are inviting him to come forward," he told a press conference at the northeast district police headquarters here today.
Those who can contact investigators.
The appeal follows a July 16 raid on a four-storey bungalow in Bukit Mertajam, where police rescued a 26-year-old Cameroonian woman who was allegedly confined by a phone scam syndicate.
A total of 29 people, including foreign nationals from the Philippines, Thailand, South Africa, Pakistan, Cambodia and Nigeria, were detained during the operation.
Lim said investigators believe the syndicate, allegedly led by a Taiwanese national, recruited foreigners to impersonate police officers in phone scam operations targeting victims in Canada and the United States.
Investigations into the phone scam syndicate are ongoing.
In a separate matter, Lim said Penang police had also intensified enforcement against illegal gambling under Op Soga XI, conducted from June 11 to July 19.
He said the operation saw 31 raids carried out, resulting in 36 arrests and the opening of 31 investigation papers.
"Twenty-four investigation papers have been brought to court. Of these, 23 cases ended in guilty pleas while one is still under trial.
"Four investigation papers are still under investigation and three were classified as requiring no further action due to insufficient evidence," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Myanmar nationals make up majority of 22,166 immigration detainees
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: A total of 22,166 undocumented migrants have been detained at 20 immigration depots nationwide, with more than half of them being Myanmar nationals, as of June 30.
Home Minister Datuk Seri Saifuddin Nasution Ismail said 11,194 detainees were from Myanmar, followed by 5,200 Filipinos, 3,247 Indonesians and 1,133 Bangladeshis.
Other nationalities included 277 from Thailand, 250 from India, 193 from Pakistan, 174 from China, 85 from Vietnam, 76 from Nepal, 49 from Cambodia and 288 from other countries.
"Of this number, 17,872 (81 per cent) were male, while 4,294 (19 per cent) were female," he said in a written parliamentary reply published on July 16.
Saifuddin said one detainee had been held for 11 years, while eight had been detained for between six and 10 years, followed by 5,035 detainees who had been held for one to five years.
He added that 356 children and female guardians were placed at seven Baitul Mahabbah centres as of June 30.
Saifuddin was responding to a question from Wong Chen (PH-Subang), who sought a breakdown of detainees at immigration depots by age, gender, nationality and duration of detention.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Lebanon PM says Beirut working for 'complete Israeli withdrawal'
Article:
ZAWTAR AL-GHARBIYAH: Beirut is working towards a "complete Israeli withdrawal" from areas it occupies, Lebanese Prime Minister Nawaf Salam said on Wednesday on a visit to a village where Lebanon's army recently deployed.
"We will continue mobilising our political and diplomatic efforts to secure a complete Israeli withdrawal from the south," he said in Zawtar al-Gharbiya, one of several "pilot zones" intended to demonstrate that the Lebanese army can remove and disarm Iran-backed Hizbollah as part of a Washington-sponsored deal with Israel.
The Lebanese military had begun deploying in the village on Tuesday. Israeli forces were previously present on its outskirts.
Salam made his statement a day after Lebanese President Joseph Aoun met with his US counterpart Donald Trump at the White House, where Aoun said he "stressed the urgent need for a full Israeli withdrawal from Lebanese territory."
Under the agreement signed last month, Lebanon's army is meant to disarm Hizbollah in the "pilot zones" it will operate in, banning military activity from any non-state group to pave the way for future Israeli withdrawals from other areas.
The group has repeatedly rejected the agreement and refuses to lay down its weapons.
However, the military said on Tuesday that Israeli forces "opened fire in the vicinity of these units," adding that the incident would hinder the deployment.
The Israeli military said it had only fired "warning shots into the air" after Lebanese soldiers entered an area that was "not part of the pilot area."
Hizbollah drew Lebanon into the Middle East war by attacking Israel in support of Teheran on March 2, prompting major Israeli airstrikes and a ground invasion.
After planting a Lebanese flag in the village, Salam said that "in parallel with the army's deployment, we will continue opening roads, removing rubble, and securing essential services, enabling our people to return safely and with dignity to their homes and villages."
Israeli strikes on Lebanon have killed more than 4,300 people since March, according to the Lebanese authorities, while Israeli forces have demolished scores of buildings in the south.
The violence has dropped off sharply since the signing of a framework agreement between the United States and Iran last month and the Lebanon-Israel deal.
While more than a million people were displaced by the violence, more than 700,000 have returned to their homes over the past few weeks, according to the United Nations.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Rebranded MAHA Pahang begins in Raub tomorrow, targets RM1.3mil sales
Article:
BERA: The rebranded Pahang Malaysia Agriculture, Horticulture and Agrotourism Exhibition (MAHA) will begin in Raub tomorrow, featuring about 100 entrepreneurs and targeting RM1.3 million in sales.
Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Wan Rosdy Wan Ismail said the four-day event, which ends on July 26, is expected to attract more than 100,000 visitors, including those from outside the state.
"This is the second edition following the rebranding of the mini-MAHA Pahang held in Temerloh last year. The inaugural programme received an overwhelming response and has now become an annual event.
"I hope that MAHA Pahang will continue to serve as a platform for promoting the state's agricultural products, showcasing innovations in the agro sector, and creating broader market opportunities for local entrepreneurs," he wrote on Facebook today.
Wan Rosdy also called on the public to appreciate the dedication of farmers, livestock breeders and agro-entrepreneurs by supporting locally produced goods and strengthening the agricultural sector as one of the state's key economic drivers.
Earlier, Wan Rosdy flagged off 25 vehicles participating in the MAHA Pahang convoy, representing various agencies and departments, including the State Secretary's Office, Pahang Agriculture Department, Veterinary Services Department, Fisheries Department, Federal Agricultural Marketing Authority and Pahang Agricultural Development Corporation, at the Bera District and Land Office.
The state-owned PahangMedia news portal reported that MAHA Pahang visitors can also participate in a lucky draw offering various prizes, including a Wagyu cow as the grand prize, a Perodua Bezza and other gifts.
Themed "Agro Lestari Bina Negeri", MAHA Pahang will be held at Dataran Raub, the Raub Silver Jubilee Hall and Taman Tasik Raub from July 23 to July 26.
Last year, Pahang introduced the mini-MAHA, a scaled-down version of the country's premier agriculture exposition, which is held biennially at the Malaysia Agro Exposition Park in Selangor.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Seoul Mayor Oh Se-hoon faces risk of losing office after political funds conviction
Article:
SEOUL: A South Korean court on Wednesday convicted Seoul Mayor Oh Se-hoon and fined him 10 million won (US$6,759) over a political funds case linked to opinion polls conducted ahead of the 2021 Seoul mayoral by-election.
The court found Oh received polling services worth 21 million won and had a longtime supporter cover the costs, in violation of the Political Funds Act, and ordered the forfeiture of 21 million won, according to a court statement.
Oh said he could not accept the ruling and would appeal.
The ruling puts Oh, who was re-elected in June, at risk of losing his office. Under South Korean law, elected officials forfeit office if they receive a fine exceeding 1 million won or a jail term.
Oh is serving a fifth term in the powerful role as Seoul mayor.
He is regarded as a leading figure in the main conservative opposition People Power Party (PPP) after President Lee Jae Myung's ruling Democratic Party swept most other major local races in elections last month.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Suspended cop charged with sexually assaulting 11-year-old daughter while on bail
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: A suspended police corporal claimed trial at the Ampang Sessions Court today to two charges of physically sexually assaulting his 11-year-old daughter in May.
The 35-year-old pleaded not guilty after the charges were read before judge Norshila Kamarudin.
The court heard that the alleged offences were committed while the accused was out on bail for a separate alleged rape case involving his 18-year-old eldest daughter.
The court was also informed that the accused is currently on psychiatric medication.
On the latest two charges, he was accused of committing the acts with sexual intent against his third daughter as a person in a position of trust as her biological father.
The alleged offences were committed at a house in Kuala Kubu Bharu in Rawang, with the first occurring between 9pm and 10pm on May 21, and the second at 2pm on May 22.
The charges were framed under Section 14(b) of the Sexual Offences Against Children Act 2016, punishable under Section 14 of the same Act, which carries a maximum sentence of 20 years' jail and whipping upon conviction
The charges were also read together with Section 16(1) of the same Act, which relates to offences committed by persons in a position of trust. If convicted, the provision carries an additional jail term of up to five years and a minimum of two strokes of the rotan.
Deputy public prosecutor Muhammad Hairuliqram Hairuddin did not offer bail.
"The accused had previously been charged at the Kuala Kubu Baru Court under Section 376 of the Penal Code for an alleged rape offence involving his eldest daughter.
"After he was granted bail, a family member of the alleged victim lodged a complaint alleging that he had harassed her.
"I ask the court not to grant bail as there are concerns that he may continue to harass the victim," he said.
However, defence lawyer Muhammad Azim Hilmi Aziz argued for bail, saying that the current charges were separate from the earlier case and that his client was prepared to comply with additional bail conditions.
"My client no longer lives with his daughters and is currently receiving psychiatric treatment at Hospital Bahagia Ulu Kinta, Perak.
"He has been prescribed medication for his condition, but he is not allowed to take it while in prison as it causes side effects.
"I request that the court allow my client to be released on bail," he said.
Hairuliqram then suggested that the accused be placed at the hospital instead, saying this would allow his condition to be monitored effectively.
However, the suggestion was objected to by Azim, who said his client did not wish to be sent to a psychiatric facility.
He said that the accused's mother was prepared to post bail and ensure his attendance at all court proceedings.
However, Norshila rejected the bail application after being informed that the accused resides in Kuala Kubu Bharu while his mother lives in Ipoh.
"She does not live with the accused. How can she ensure that he attends every court proceeding in the future?
"Furthermore, there have been complaints from the victim's family regarding harassment.
"At this stage, I will not allow bail until the defence provides a surety who can ensure the accused's attendance in court and compliance with the condition that he does not interfere with the victim," she ruled.
The judge said that the court would consider the defence's bail application at the next mention date, which has been fixed for Aug 26.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: More tremors, another inspection: Inti building declared safe
Article:
SUBANG JAYA: Another building was declared safe after members of the public reported feeling tremors following the
earthquake in northern Sumatra
today.
The Fire and Rescue Department inspected a building at Inti International College Subang after receiving a report at 12.40pm.
A team arrived three minutes later to assess the building for possible cracks or structural damage.
The department said its operations commander received information from members of the public regarding the reported tremor.
The inspection was completed at 12.58pm, with firefighters finding no cracks or structural damage. The building was subsequently declared safe for occupation.
Five firefighters, assisted by one Fire Rescue Tender (FRT), were deployed for the operation.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Woman dies after losing contact with group during Pulau Tioman dive
Article:
KUANTAN, July 22 — A local woman died while diving in the waters off Kampung Salang, Pulau Tioman, last Sunday.
Rompin police chief Supt Sharif Shai Sharif Mondoi said the victim, Heng Jing Ying, 31, had taken part in the ‘Aware Coral’ activity organised by Tioman Dive Buddy Centre, Kampung Genting, from July 17 to 18.
He said on July 19, the victim, together with nine other participants, an instructor and a dive master, joined a fun dive at Seafan, Batu Malang, Pulau Tulai.
“Before the activity, the participants were briefed on safety procedures and equipment usage, although they were licensed divers,” he said in a statement today.
Sharif Shai said the victim was believed to have separated from the group while at a depth of 17.6 metres, leading the instructor to conduct a search.
“After almost 30 minutes, the victim was found on the sandy seabed not far from the diving site,” he said.
He said the victim was given CPR after being brought onto the boat but showed no response before being taken to Kampung Tekek Clinic, where she was pronounced dead.
Her body was later sent to the Forensic Unit of Tengku Ampuan Afzan Hospital (HTAA) for a post-mortem.
Sharif Shai said the post-mortem found that she died from arterial gas embolism (AGE), a condition where gas or air bubbles enter the arterial bloodstream.
Police investigations found no criminal elements involved, and the case was classified as sudden death. — Bernama

Title: Kidnap victim rescued in RM7m ransom case detained over alleged drug trafficking links
Article:
SUBANG JAYA, July 22 — A kidnap victim rescued within 48 hours after police foiled a RM7 million ransom plot has been detained over alleged involvement in syndicated drug trafficking activities, federal police said today.
Bukit Aman Criminal Investigation Department director Commissioner Datuk M. Kumar said the victim was detained under the Dangerous Drugs (Special Preventive Measures) Act after the Narcotics Criminal Investigation Department uncovered alleged links to drug-related activities on July 21.
“Further checks on employment records and Employees Provident Fund (EPF) contributions also failed to reveal a fixed occupation for the victim,” he told a press conference at the Subang Jaya police headquarters here.
Apart from the victim, Kumar said two other suspects were also arrested, bringing the total number of individuals detained in connection with the case to eight.
He added that one of the suspects was a former policeman who had been dismissed several years ago for a drug-related offence.
Previously, six individuals — five men and one woman — were arrested after the victim was rescued from a residential unit in i-City, Shah Alam, on July 18.

Title: Sabah Assembly passes sweeping Native Court reforms, introduces new three-tier system
Article:
KOTA KINABALU, July 22 — Sabah today passed Bills for sweeping reforms to its traditional Native Court system introducing a new legal framework aimed at modernising customary justice through clearer court structures, merit-based judicial appointments, appeals, legal representation and even remote hearings.
The Native Courts Bill 2026, tabled by State local government and housing minister Datuk Ariffin Arif aims to replace the Native Courts Enactment 1992 which he said had been in force for more than 30 years and no longer adequately reflected changes in governance, public expectations and the state’s socio-economic development.
“The objective of this Bill is not to change, standardise or abolish customary laws,” he said in his winding-up speech.
“Customs remain alive within the community and differ according to each ethnic group, district and cultural background. What is being reformed is the institution and administration of customary justice so that customs can continue to be preserved and implemented through a more orderly, fair and credible court system.”
Among the most significant changes is the restructuring of Sabah’s Native Court hierarchy into three tiers — the District Native Court, the High Native Court and the Native Court of Appeal — providing clearer judicial responsibilities and a more structured appeals process.
The Bill also clearly defines the court’s jurisdiction by setting out which matters fall under the Native Courts while reaffirming that civil and Syariah court jurisdictions remain unaffected.
“The purpose is to create legal certainty, reduce overlapping jurisdictions and ensure disputes are heard by the appropriate forum,” Ariffin said.
To strengthen judicial independence, the proposed law establishes a Native Judicial Appointments Advisory Committee responsible for recommending judges based on merit, qualifications, knowledge of customary laws, experience and integrity.
The Bill also introduces clearer provisions governing judges’ qualifications, tenure, appointment procedures, removal from office and a judicial code of ethics aimed at improving professionalism and accountability.
Another key reform is the formal recognition of Native Court Counsel, allowing parties appearing before higher Native Courts to obtain legal representation during proceedings.
The legislation also modernises court administration by creating new positions including Chief Registrar, Registrar and Assistant Registrar to improve case management, court records and enforcement of orders.
Recognising the importance of customary knowledge, the Bill establishes a formal Register of Elders, enabling qualified traditional elders to assist the courts in cases involving indigenous customs.
To improve access to justice, particularly for Sabah’s rural and interior communities, the government is also proposing circuit courts that can travel to different districts, alongside provisions allowing proceedings to be conducted through remote communication technology where appropriate.
Ariffin said the reforms would be implemented gradually, taking into account manpower, funding, infrastructure and training requirements while ensuring ongoing cases under the 1992 enactment would continue without disruption.
He said the reforms would ultimately benefit the public by improving the efficiency and consistency of Native Court decisions while strengthening confidence in the institution while continuing to preserve indigenous customs.
The Bill was among four tabled and eventually passed by the State Legislative Assembly today. The others were the Islamic Inheritance Bill 2026, Sabah Ports Authority (Amendment) Bill 2026 and Sabah Ports (Privatisation) (Amendment) Bill 2026.

Title: US-Iran conflict flares up again but Malaysian businesses stay prepared with measures in place
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, July 22 — The renewed escalation of tensions in West Asia has once again rattled global markets, renewing concerns over one of the world’s major geopolitical flashpoints and its potential impact on global trade.
For many Malaysian businesses, however, the latest developments merely reinforce the need to remain vigilant rather than return to business as usual.
Manufacturers, retailers and small businesses continue to operate in an environment shaped by volatile shipping costs, fluctuating energy prices and fragile supply chains that have persisted after geopolitical tensions first emerged.
Businesses recognise that global logistics networks and supply chains will take much longer to recover even if tensions ease.
That has prompted many companies to rethink how they operate, from diversifying suppliers and improving inventory management to strengthening financial planning and building greater operational resilience.
The shift reflects a broader lesson from the past few years that started with the pandemic: resilience has become just as important as efficiency.
It has become part of everyday business strategy — one built on diversification, prudent planning and the understanding that uncertainty is likely to remain a defining feature of the global economy for the foreseeable future.
Tan Sri Hassan Marican, chairman of the Crisis Management Task Force (PPPK) under the National Economic Action Council (MTEN), warned in an interview with Bernama in June that energy disruptions extend far beyond fuel prices and could eventually affect food supplies, medical products and manufacturing.
“The effects of energy disruptions can spill over into many sectors because energy is a key input throughout the economy,” he said, adding that businesses should prepare for prolonged uncertainty rather than expect supply chains to return quickly to pre-crisis conditions.
Hassan also stressed that Malaysia’s relative stability during recent global disruptions was not by chance, but the result of early intervention by the government to cushion businesses and consumers from external shocks.
Among the measures introduced were targeted assistance, continued monitoring of essential supplies, logistics improvements and enforcement against profiteering to help maintain market stability despite external pressures.
However, Hassan emphasised that government action alone is insufficient.
He added that national resilience requires a “whole-of-nation” approach, with businesses and consumers also playing an active role in adapting to prolonged global uncertainty.
For businesses, that means moving beyond short-term responses by strengthening supply chain planning, reviewing procurement strategies, managing costs more efficiently and building flexibility into operations to better withstand future disruptions.
An example of a business that pivoted quickly to meet such challenges is Dking, a durian online store that has been around for 17 years.
In an interview with
Malay Mail
in April, Leron Yee who is one of the founders said “we can still try to reduce whatever costs that we are able to.”
He explained that the company adopted a more strategic approach to workforce planning while also optimising their internal budget by taking one per cent off across all departments.
Businesses are not facing these challenges alone. To complement such efforts, the government has rolled out a RM15 billion support package to help MSMEs weather global uncertainties, including expanded loan guarantees, the SME Stabilisation Relief Facility and additional microfinancing for small businesses and traders.
As of June 25, about RM1 billion had been approved for 1,500 SMEs under the relief facility.
The Federation of Malaysian Manufacturers welcomed the facility and efforts to help companies source alternative raw materials, while noting that mid-tier manufacturers facing high freight, insurance and energy costs required further assistance.
Businesses and the public can also monitor developments through the government’s Global Supply Crisis dashboard, which tracks key indicators affecting supply chains,logistics and market conditions.
While the West Asia peace deal represents a welcome step towards greater geopolitical stability, businesses acknowledge that global supply chains remain interconnected and vulnerable to disruptions beyond any single region.
For Malaysian companies, resilience which began as a response to this and previous crises represents a mind shift which only makes them stronger.

Title: Police bust RM27m-a-day World Cup gambling syndicate in Klang Valley
Article:
SUBANG JAYA, July 22 — A major online betting syndicate with an estimated RM27 million in daily betting transactions has been dismantled by federal police following a series of coordinated operations targeting online gambling activities during the 2026 Fifa World Cup recently.
Bukit Aman Criminal Investigation Department director Commissioner Datuk M Kumar said a series of raids were conducted following investigations by the Malaysian Communications and Multimedia Commission (MCMC) to identify the owners of websites promoting and advertising gambling activities on social media platforms.
He said police raided 19 business and residential premises linked to the syndicate across the Klang Valley on July 16, resulting in the arrest of 161 individuals — 106 men and 55 women, including six foreigners aged between 19 and 62 — suspected of being involved in the syndicate’s activities over the past six months.
